author: maintainer-agent

patch.value:
Minor deviations from current official plugin docs (code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference, fetched 2026-07-11; Claude Code v2.1.207). None block loading — `claude plugin validate --strict` passes today. Sweep these when touching the plugin (the substantive install-path defect is pm-l9wl):

1. `plugins/pm-claude/.mcp.json` carries a non-schema `"note"` key inside the server entry. Unrecognized-field tolerance is documented for plugin.json top level, not for MCP server configs; Claude Code settings validators are known to strip unknown keys. Move the note to a JS/README comment.
2. `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` nests `description`/`version` under `metadata` — docs mark that "accepted for backward compatibility"; best practice is top-level fields.
3. `commands/` (14 flat .md) is the legacy form — docs: "Commands: Skills as flat Markdown files. Use `skills/` for new plugins." Plan a consolidation into `skills/` (each becomes `<name>/SKILL.md`), keeping invocation names stable.
4. Adopt newer manifest fields: `$schema` (https://json.schemastore.org/claude-code-plugin-manifest.json) for editor validation, `displayName` (v2.1.143+) for the /plugin picker.
5. Root `marketplace.json` duplicate: kept intentionally for backwards compat (smoke test asserts it). Document the dual-file invariant where it's enforced, or drop it once compat window closes — it is a silent-drift hazard (only `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` is read by /plugin marketplace add).
6. If a rename/removal ever happens, use the top-level `renames` map (v2.1.193+) instead of breaking installs.
Claude plugin spec-alignment hygiene vs 2026-07-11 official docs (minor, non-blocking)

message: Filed from Claude-plugin implementation research vs official docs 2026-07-11
